description: §39.5.1 no-primary-key warning — the `logs` table declares only `message` and `created_at` columns, with no `primary key`. §39.5.1: a `<schema>` table declaration with no `primary key` column SHALL generate a compile warning (W-SCHEMA-001). The gauntlet declaration check inspects each parsed table's columns and emits W-SCHEMA-001 (severity warning → routes to result.warnings, non-fatal, CLI exit unchanged) when none is marked `primary key`. This case pins the code as a WARNING, not an error.
rationale: §39.12 error table + §39.5.1 normative statement: a `<schema>` table declaration with no `primary key` column SHALL generate a compile warning (W-SCHEMA-001: table has no primary key). The severity assertion guards the diagnostic-stream partition — a W- code lands in the warnings stream, never the fatal errors stream.
